observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

   We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the  Inventory
   Committee to reconcile property and accounting records as provided  under
   the Handbook on PSMS, to eliminate doubt on the existence, condition  and
   correctness of the total value of property, plant and equipment owned  by
   the Municipality amounting to P153,325,758.90.


   We also recommended the following procedures:


   1. Physically count all existing PPE and provide its description.  Secure
      other information such as person accountable, date of purchase, brand,
      model, serial no., engine no., etc. and determine if unserviceable  or
      not;



   2. Based on the information gathered in procedure  no.  1,  determine  if
      there are accounting records for these PPEs.  Create a PPELC for  each
      PPE with accounting records or data, identify and determine its  total
      cost and  compute  a  separate  depreciation  schedule.   PPE  without
      accounting records shall be properly disclosed;



   3. Identify the accountable employee for each PPE and  issue  a  Property
      Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR);

   4. For the current purchases of PPE,  immediately  prepare  a  PPELC  and
      issue a PAR to the employee.


   Furthermore, we recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  a
   journal entry for the recognition of 25% of Public Infrastructures for CY
   2016.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials  and
   employees to  liquidate  and/or  refund  immediately  their  unused  cash
   advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and COA
   Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, to avoid  accumulation  of  unliquidated
   cash advances.

   We also recommended that the LCE refrain from granting cash  advances  to
   officials and employees with  unliquidated  cash  advances  and  strictly
   adhere to the guidelines in granting cash advances.

   We further recommended for the imposition  of  appropriate  sanctions  to
   those concerned officials and employees who did not liquidate their  cash
   advances.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all
   her collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily  or  not
   later than the next banking day as required under Section 69  of  PD  No.
   1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.


   We also recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the  Municipal
   Treasurer reconcile their records to determine  the  correctness  of  the
   account.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that a request be made  to  the  depository  bank  by  the
   Municipal Treasurer and the LCE, as soon as  possible,  to  furnish  them
   copies of Credit Memos and Debit Memos in order for these transactions to
   be  examined  and  recognized  if  they  are  indeed  valid  and   proper
   transactions of the  Municipality.  Also,  identified  erroneous  charges
   shall be clarified with the bank for correction by the latter.


   We also recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  make  the  necessary
   adjustments  on  the  identified  reconciling  items  with  complete  and
   validated supporting attachments, by preparing the journal entry vouchers
   to correct the balances of the  Cash  in  Bank-LCCA  and  other  affected
   accounts.

Recommendation:

   We recommended that the  LCE  with  the  support  of  LGU  officials  and
   personnel fast track the implementation of  the  Municipal  Ordinance  to
   secure source of fund for the full implementation of the SWM Program.

Recommendation:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that
   SET Shares of the Iloilo Provincial Government are  released  within  the
   reglementary period in compliance of Section 9 of RA  No.  5447  for  the
   timely use of funds for its Provincial School Board Budget.

Recommendation:

   We recommended that the LCE direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  OIC-
   Municipal Treasurer to coordinate in facilitating the remittance  of  the
   PDAF balances to the National Treasury.

Recommendation:

   We recommended that the LCE with the support of  the  Municipal  Engineer
   and Municipal Planning and Development Officer strictly comply  with  the
   provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004  on  the  submission  of  various
   reports on Government PPAs, information and publicity of  such   PPAs  to
   promote good governance through transparency and  accountability  to  the
   public.

Recommendations:

   We   recommended   that   the    Municipal    Accountant    record    the
   unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the  DRRMF-MOOE  under  the
   account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books as provided  under
   COA Circular No. 2012-002.  Necessary adjusting entries shall be effected
   to correct the original accounting entries made.

Recommendation:

   We recommended that the SEF be solely and exclusively used  for  programs
   and activities embodied in RA Nos. 5447 and 7160 to maximize the benefits
   for the students in the Municipality of Barotac Nuevo.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the preparation/submission  of  a  GAD
   Plan and Budget  and  accomplishment  report  and  observe  strictly  the
   provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that all development projects shall be implemented  during
   the year as embodied in the Annual Development Plan.


   We likewise recommended that disbursements for the 20%  Development  Fund
   shall be in accordance with the guidelines set in the  DILG-DBM  JMC  No.
   2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the full settlement of the subject  ND
   and implement the proper action in undertaking the settlement or  request
   the approval of installment payments of the ND from the COA.


   We also recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  recognize/record  the
   said disallowance in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the persons  responsible  to  properly
   observe the provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.  96-010  relative  to  the
   information needed to be shown in the purchase orders and ensure that the
   quality and specifications of the items delivered correspond to the value
   paid by the agency and essentially meet the needs of the end users.

Recommendation:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the  concerned  personnel  to  observe
   strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and  Section  6  of
   COA Circular No. 95-006 on the timely, complete and proper submission  of
   required reports.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads  to
   submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2015 AAR
   findings   and   recommendations   including   that   of   prior   years,
   related/connected to  their  departments  and  the  information  gathered
   therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same  to
   the COA Auditor.


   We also recommended that the LCE submit the properly accomplished  AAPSI,
   within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the AAR, to the COA,  either
   in printed form or by way of electronic document.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their unused cash advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.  We also recommended that the LCE refrain from granting cash advances to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances and strictly adhere to the guidelines in granting cash advances.  We further recommended for the imposition of appropriate sanctions for the unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances.
observations.reason: Cash advances amounting to P11,680,454.39 remained outstanding as of December 31, 2016. P12,928,639.65 is the accountability of the previous Municipal Treasurer (deceased). Reiterated in this year’s Report.  Not Implemented Cash advances were still granted to employees and officials with existing unliquidated CA.  Not Implemented Recommended sanctions were not imposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, Management still continued to maintain a significant amount of cash in the local treasury. Reiterated in this year’s Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all development projects be utilized during the year as embodied in the Annual Development Plan.  We likewise recommended that disbursements for the 20% Development Fund (DF) be in accordance with the guidelines set in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.reason: Unimplemented projects totalled to P57,998,066.01 or 88.65% as of 12/31/2016. Reiterated in this year’s Report.  Partially Implemented For CY 2016, Management still had some expenses incurred contrary to the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the persons concerned to observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Section A.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 by submitting a copy of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from its execution and notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of such deliveries.
observations.reason: Submission was not within the prescribed period. Reiterated in this year’s Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the preparation/submission of a GAD Plan and Budget and the creation of its Municipal GFPS that will spearhead the execution and reporting of the annual GPB and GAD AR, respectively, and observe strictly the provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: No GAD plan and budget and its accomplishment report were prepared for CYs 2015-2016. Reiterated in this year’s Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments to properly classify the accounts based on the account description provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit subsidiary ledgers or detailed schedules of her working papers on the conversion of accounts to ascertain its correctness.
observations.reason:
No adjusting entries were presented.
Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) require the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to consolidate the PPMPs into the APP, using the standard form so that procurement could be undertaken accordingly, taking into account the allowable period for the procurement processes so that goods will be available at the time needed, avail of discounts, if any, on bulk procurement and minimize reimbursements for unplanned purchases.

We recommended that the transfer of funds to other LGUs covering the implementation of certain programs/projects of the Municipality of Barotac Nuevo must be covered by an Approved Program of Work or Approved Project Expenditures, MOA and other documentary requirements enumerated under Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to provide basis for monitoring the proper use of funds. Also, transfer of funds shall be recorded as Due from LGUs so that liquidation from the recipient-Barangays will be required and any unutilized balance refunded.
